description_long: This is a small, traditional Japanese-style pagoda structure with a prominent, wide, dark green-tiled hip roof that overhangs the building on all sides. The roof appears weathered, with visible streaks and patches, and is supported by wooden posts and railings that enclose a raised stone platform. The front features a set of stone steps leading up to a pair of red double doors, flanked by decorative wooden lattice windows and a simple wooden balustrade. The walls are primarily light gray stone or plaster, adorned with geometric wooden trim and subtle greenish stains, suggesting age and exposure to the elements. The structure sits on a rectangular stone base, with the roof forming the most visually dominant element. There are no visible ornamental finials or upward-curving eaves, and the building is a single-story construction.
description: A small, weathered Japanese-style pagoda with a wide, dark green-tiled roof, wooden posts, and red double doors on a raised stone platform.
description_view_0: Front view showing the stone steps, red double doors, and decorative wooden lattice windows.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the overhanging roof and the wooden posts supporting the structure.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain wall with geometric wooden trim and the full width of the roof.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the wooden posts and the stone platform extending outward.
